summary: Adenosine signaling has been shown to have significant prognostic implications specifically within adrenocortical carcinoma, where it correlates with aggressive disease features such as TP53 mutations and β-catenin dysregulation leading to poorer survival outcomes. This indicates context-dependent prognostic relevance rather than broad applicability across all malignancies.
